metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

**agent (yes).** Gemini CLI is a tool-using agent: the model emits function calls (`toolCalls`) for built-in tools (run_shell_command, read_file, write_file, etc.), the runtime executes them, and feeds `functionResponse` results back into the next turn — all inside one user turn, no second prompt. Run under `gemini -y` (yolo) the tool calls auto-approve with no confirmation gate, which is exactly this scenario's gate-less happy path. A prompt like "run echo hello via the shell tool, then reply done" exercises run_shell_command directly.

**daemon (full).** Confirmed against the live transcript fixture `core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/testdata/real-toolcall-session.jsonl`, which contains the full arc the daemon parses: a `gemini` assistant message carrying `toolCalls` (parser.go parseAssistant → ev.ToolUses, content empty/tool-calling so the session stays `working`), a user-role message of `functionResponse` parts (parser.go parseUser → EventType `function_call_output`, closes the open tool WITHOUT ClearToolNames/settle), then a final `gemini` message with non-empty content and zero toolCalls (parser.go:209 → EventType `turn_done`, settling working→ready). The opening user prompt drives ready→working (parser.go parseUser → `user_message`). So the daemon emits ready→working (prompt), holds working across the tool round-trip, and working→ready on the terminal text turn — the scenario's `ready → working → ready` arc with a tool call inside the working span. Nothing in the auto-approved path sets PermissionPending, so `waiting` is unreachable, satisfying bullet 2.

**driver (ready).** The recipe needs only `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep`, all implemented by `replaydata/agents/gemini-cli/driver-interactive.sh` (DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"). The driver launches `gemini -y` (yolo auto-approves so no gate trips), auto-accepts the first-run trust-folder prompt, and `wait_turn` blocks on the transcript turn count — mirroring the parser's heuristic turn-end classification. No missing step type.

## What the daemon does NOT surface (and why that's OK)

Individual tool_use / function_call_output pairs are parsed and counted but never flicker session state — by design intra-turn tool calls keep the session `working`. The spec asserts only state-level observables, which is what the daemon emits.
caveats: Gemini emits NO explicit end-of-turn marker; the parser heuristically settles working→ready when an assistant message has non-empty content and zero toolCalls (parser.go:209). The turn-end edge is therefore emission-dependent — this is a doc/code+fixture verdict, PROVISIONAL on the exact streaming-flush ordering of a live run; record promotes it from provisional to settled.
A tool call that tripped an interactive approval would push state to waiting (different feature). The recipe runs under `gemini -y` (yolo) so every tool auto-approves and waiting stays unreachable.

Tool-call visibility is coarse in the transcript (call id + name, plus the functionResponse close); the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the auto-executed tool call is asserted via the single uninterrupted working span plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase.
RECORDING PREREQ: Gemini OAuth (oauth-personal) must be set up out-of-band; the Google One / unpaid tier stops serving on 2026-06-18, after which recordings on those tiers fail auth.

sources.note: parseUser: user_message drives ready→working, functionResponse → function_call_output closes tool without settling; parseAssistant: toolCalls → ToolUses keeps working, non-empty content + zero toolCalls (line 209) → turn_done settles working→ready.

Live transcript fixture: 1 gemini message with toolCalls, 2 functionResponse user parts, 1 terminal gemini text message with no toolCalls — the exact ready→working→ready arc with a tool call mid-turn.
Adapter declares FilesUnderRoot(~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats) + JSONLineParser; commandPattern bin/gemini; isHeapBumpWorker excludes the V8 heap-bump re-exec so one launcher session is observed.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" covers the recipe; launches `gemini -y` (yolo auto-approve), auto-accepts trust-folder, wait_turn blocks on transcript turn count.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth-personal), set up out-of-band
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_HOME + alt port so prod is untouched)
Driver runs `gemini -y` (yolo) so the shell tool auto-approves and no permission gate trips
setup: Driver stages a fresh cwd and launches gemini -y under tmux, auto-accepting the first-run trust-folder prompt
wait_turn resolves the rollout transcript created after launch and blocks on its turn count

script.text: Run the shell command 'echo hello' using the run_shell_command tool, then reply 'done'.

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready, then exactly one ready→working transition for the session
events.jsonl shows exactly one working→ready transition (the terminal text turn)
events.jsonl contains at least one tool_use event (run_shell_command) inside the working span
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting for the session during the entire run
a single process_exited on the session after the turn
